name: Sweet Chocolate Cake/1953 Pillsbury Bakeoff
recipeInstructions:
A winner in the senior division of the 1953 Pillsbury Bakeoff.
Cake:
1. Mix together flour, baking powder, and salt; set aside.
2. Cream shortening and sugar well.
3. Beat egg yolks for 1 minute, then add to shortening mixture along with milk and vanilla. Blend well.
4. Add dry ingredients and beat until smooth.
5. Beat egg whites until stiff.
6. Melt chocolate over low heat.
7. Add melted chocolate to batter and mix well.
8. Fold in beaten egg whites.
9. Pour batter into 2 well-greased, lightly floured 8-inch round cake pans.
10. Bake preheated oven at 350 degrees for 25-30 minutes.
11. Cool for a few minutes, then remove cake to finish cooling on a wire rack.
Chocolate Cream Cheese Frosting:
1. Blend together softened cream cheese, 3 tablespoons milk, and salt.
2. Add confectioners' sugar gradually, beating well.
3. Blend in melted chocolate and vanilla.
4. Beat until smooth. Thin with an additional 1-2 teaspoons of milk if necessary for the right spreading consistency.
5. Frost between cake layers and on tops and sides.
